rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by adding missing annex blocks or fixing invalid references
Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantees
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Use valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for const and eq declarations
generic_rules: Repair only the specific diagnostic issue without changing unrelated architecture
Use named constants or visible relations for assumptions and guarantees when possible
Trace unresolved identifiers to their root cause before adding architecture
Delete invalid clauses instead of adding architecture when the root cause is unresolved
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not place implementation-level references in component type annexes
Do not use invalid AGREE scalar type names like 'integer' or 'boolean'
